nodes.x: When therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,
Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?

from the Gospel that it was usual among the Jews to admit men to the profession of a doctrine by baptism ; for the Pharisees do not find fault with John’s baptism, but only blame him for baptizing when he was neither the Messiah, nor Elias, nor that Prophet. "When, therefore, this forerunner of the Messiah baptized such persons as he disposed and prepared to receive him, he did no more than…
¶ Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.
(Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples,)
(For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
And he said unto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they said, Unto John’s baptism.
And he came up out of the water and began to baptize. And he baptized all those whom Jesus had chosen.
And he that believeth and is baptized shall be saved, and he that believeth not, and is not baptized, shall be damned.
He left Judæa, and departed again into Galilee.
¶ Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these sayings, he departed from Galilee, and came into the coasts of Judæa beyond Jordan;
When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judæa into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.
But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judæa,
Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judæa again.
And he must needs go through Samaria.
For the saying which he cried by the word of the Lord against the altar in Beth-el, and against all the houses of the high places which are in the cities of Samaria, shall surely come to pass.
¶ And Elisha said unto them, This is not the way, neither is this the city: follow me, and I will bring you to the man whom ye seek. But he led them to Samaria.
And all the people shall know, even Ephraim and the inhabitant of Samaria, that say in the pride and stoutness of heart,
And the king of Israel went to his house heavy and displeased, and came to Samaria.
¶ And he arose and departed, and came to Samaria. And as he was at the shearing house in the way,
Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
And Isaac sent away Jacob: and he went to Padan-aram unto Laban, son of Bethuel the Syrian, the brother of Rebekah, Jacob’s and Esau’s mother.
Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
Then Jacob went on his journey, and came into the land of the people of the east.
And Jacob called the name of the place where God spake with him, Beth-el.
And that Jacob obeyed his father and his mother, and was gone to Padan-aram;
Now Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well: and it was about the sixth hour.

XLIV. And Israel took his journey from †Haran† 1 from his house on the new moon of the third month, and he went on the way of the Well of the Oath, 2 and he offered a sacrifice to the God of his father Isaac on the seventh of this month. 2. And Jacob remembered the dream that he had seen at Bethel, 3 and he feared to go down into Egypt. 3. And while he was thinking of sending word to Joseph to…

1. As soon as Jacob came to know, by his sons returning home, in what
state Joseph was, that he had not only escaped death, for which yet he
lived all along in mourning, but that he lived in splendor and
happiness, and ruled over Egypt, jointly with the king, and had
intrusted to his care almost all his affairs, he did not think any thing
he was told to be incredible, considering the greatness of…
And Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
Alas! for that day is great, so that none is like it: it is even the time of Jacob’s trouble; but he shall be saved out of it.
¶ And Jacob awaked out of his sleep, and he said, Surely the Lord is in this place; and I knew it not.
There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.

Living Water to Quench Spiritual Thirst By Elder Joseph B. Wirthlin Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles Early in his mortal ministry, the Savior and his disciples passed through Samaria while traveling from Judea to Galilee. Weary, hungry, and thirsty from their journey, they stopped at Jacob’s well in the city of Sychar. While the disciples went in search of food, the Savior remained at the…
The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.

which  am  a  woman  of  Samaria?  for  the  Jews  have  no  deal- 
ings with  the  Samaritans."  Jesus,  seemingly  forgetful  of

would  have  given  thee  living  water."  The …
Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.

Come Drink the Living Water By Elder A. Theodore Tuttle Of the First Council of the Seventy There is a large relief sculpture on the wall of the church I attended as a boy. It depicts the Savior and the woman of Samaria at Jacob’s well. As a little boy, I pondered on the tree, the well, the Savior, the woman, the city in the distance, and the approach of the disciples. As I grew older, I learned…
(For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)
¶ And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisee’s house, and sat down to meat.
And when he had brought them into his house, he set meat before them, and rejoiced, believing in God with all his house.
And as he entered the city he was an hungered, and he said to a man: Will ye give to an humble servant of God something to eat?
And when he had received meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certain days with the disciples which were at Damascus.
He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he will ever be mindful of his covenant.
Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.
There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.
Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.
Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:
And he said unto her, Give me, I pray thee, a little water to drink; for I am thirsty. And she opened a bottle of milk, and gave him drink, and covered him.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
Behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to pass, that when the virgin cometh forth to draw water, and I say to her, Give me, I pray thee, a little water of thy pitcher to drink;
Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?

And I will feed them that oppress thee with their own flesh; they shall be drunken with their own blood as with sweet wine; and all flesh shall know that I, the Lord, am thy Savior and thy Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.

And I will feed them that oppress thee with their own flesh; and they shall be drunken with their own blood, as with sweet wine: and all flesh shall know that I the Lord am thy Saviour and thy Redeemer, the mighty One of Jacob.

And I will feed them that oppress thee, with their own flesh; and they shall be drunken with their own blood as with sweet wine; and all flesh shall know that I the Lord am thy Savior and thy Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
Nevertheless, Jacob, my firstborn in the wilderness, thou knowest the greatness of God; and he shall consecrate thine afflictions for thy gain.
Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:

Drink of the Pure Water By President Loren C. Dunn Of the First Council of the Seventy Consider these words of the Savior as he spoke to the woman of Samaria at Jacob’s well: “Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again: “But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into…
But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.

and he would have given thee living water.” “Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.” Did the world know who these Elders are that go forth among them bearing the precious seed of eternal life; could they…
Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.
But unto him that keepeth my commandments I will give the mysteries of my kingdom, and the same shall be in him a well of living water, springing up unto everlasting life.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.
And she called unto her husband, and said, Send me, I pray thee, one of the young men, and one of the asses, that I may run to the man of God, and come again.
Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am he.
Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
And she said unto her husband, Behold now, I perceive that this is an holy man of God, which passeth by us continually.
The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no husband:
And she said unto her servants, Go on before me; behold, I come after you. But she told not her husband Nabal.
¶ His disciples say unto him, If the case of the man be so with his wife, it is not good to marry.

¶ Then the woman came and told her husband, saying, A man of God came unto me, and his countenance was like the countenance of an angel of God, very terrible: but I asked him not whence he was, neither told he me his name:
And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel’s,
For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.
But if thou hast gone aside to another instead of thy husband, and if thou be defiled, and some man have lain with thee beside thine husband:
But this I say, brethren, the time is short: it remaineth, that both they that have wives be as though they had none;
Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.
Thou shalt not take thee a wife, neither shalt thou have sons or daughters in this place.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.
And she said unto him: The servants of my husband have made it known unto me that thou art a prophet of a holy God, and that thou hast power to do many mighty works in his name;
She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
Thus saith the Lord God; Art thou he of whom I have spoken in old time by my servants the prophets of Israel, which prophesied in those days many years that I would bring thee against them?
Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.
Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.

But if ye say unto me, We trust in the Lord our God: is not that he, whose high places and whose altars Hezekiah hath taken away, and hath said to Judah and Jerusalem, Ye shall worship before this altar in Jerusalem?
We claim the privilege of worshiping Almighty God according to the dictates of our own conscience, and allow all men the same privilege, let them worship how, where, or what they may.
We claim the privilege of worshipping Almighty God according to the dictates of our conscience, and allow all men the same privilege let them worship how, where, or what they may.
Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.
Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.
But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.
Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.
Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.
Behold I say unto you, do ye suppose that ye cannot worship God save it be in your synagogues only?

How to Worship By President Bruce R. McConkie Of the First Council of the Seventy I desire to give some rather plain and affirmative counsel as to how to worship the Lord. There is probably more misinformation and error in this field than in any other area in the entire world, and yet there is no other thing as important as knowing who and how we should worship. When the Lord created men and…
I know thy works, and tribulation, and poverty, (but thou art rich) and I know the blasphemy of them which say they are Jews, and are not, but are the synagogue of Satan.
But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.
And it shall come to pass, that from one new moon to another, and from one sabbath to another, shall all flesh come to worship before me, saith the Lord.
And it shall come to pass that the time cometh, when the fulness of my gospel shall be preached unto them;
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.

For in that day, for my sake shall the Father work a work, which shall be a great and a marvelous work among them; and there shall be among them those who will not believe it, although a man shall declare it unto them.
God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
Hereby know ye the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God:

We have assembled here today to worship God our Heavenly Father, in the name of Jesus Christ His Son, under the influence and power of the Holy Spirit. In order that our worship may be acceptable to God, it must be done in the name of Jesus, and it must be done under the influence of His Spirit; for “God is a spirit, and they that worship Him must worship Him in spirit and in truth.” We must be…
Holy, holy God; we believe that thou art God, and we believe that thou art holy, and that thou wast a spirit, and that thou art a spirit, and that thou wilt be a spirit forever.
The woman saith unto him, I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things.
And said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.
He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.
Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken these things unto her.
Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am he.
He said moreover, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And she said, Say on.
And he sware unto her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask of me, I will give it thee, unto the half of my kingdom.
¶ And upon this came his disciples, and marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?
But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.
Woe unto him that saith unto his father, What begettest thou? or to the woman, What hast thou brought forth?
And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?

And they sent out unto him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest thou for any man: for thou regardest not the person of men.
But he said unto her, Thou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh. What? shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil? In all this did not Job sin with his lips.
The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men,
And the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a flood after the woman, that he might cause her to be carried away of the flood.
So she went from him, and shut the door upon her and upon her sons, who brought the vessels to her; and she poured out.
And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; and she went, and filled the bottle with water, and gave the lad drink.
Then came the woman in the dawning of the day, and fell down at the door of the man’s house where her lord was, till it was light.
But there was a strong tower within the city, and thither fled all the men and women, and all they of the city, and shut it to them, and gat them up to the top of the tower.
Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?
And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.
And I said unto him: Deniest thou the Christ who shall come? And he said: If there should be a Christ, I would not deny him; but I know that there is no Christ, neither has been, nor ever will be.
And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:
Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not.
Then they went out of the city, and came unto him.
And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.
Then said Saul to his servant, Well said; come, let us go. So they went unto the city where the man of God was.
And he said unto them, Defile the house, and fill the courts with the slain: go ye forth. And they went forth, and slew in the city.
And they came and besought them, and brought them out, and desired them to depart out of the city.
And he said unto them, Come again unto me after three days. And the people departed.
¶ In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.
Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?
¶ Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
¶ And it came to pass, as he was alone praying, his disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, Whom say the people that I am?
¶ And he said unto his disciples, Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat; neither for the body, what ye shall put on.
And when the disciples had come with bread and wine, he took of the bread and brake and blessed it; and he gave unto the disciples and commanded that they should eat.
But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.
But he said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they said, We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; except we should go and buy meat for all this people.
¶ And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.
And they said unto him, Nay, my lord, but to buy food are thy servants come.
And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?
¶ In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.
And it came to pass that Jesus commanded his disciples that they should bring forth some bread and wine unto him.
And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?
Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.
Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them.
But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.
And this I have commanded to be done for your salvation, and also for their salvation, in consequence of their being driven out and that which is to come.

I will even give them into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of them that seek their life: and their dead bodies shall be for meat unto the fowls of the heaven, and to the beasts of the earth.
Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.
For behold, the field is white already to harvest; and it is the eleventh hour, and the last time that I shall call laborers into my vineyard.

3. For behold, the field is white already to harvest: and it is the
    eleventh hour, and the last time that I shall call laborers into my
    vineyard.
Yea, verily, verily, I say unto you, that the field is white already to harvest; wherefore, thrust in your sickles, and reap with all your might, mind, and strength.

hearts of the children of men. It is said, the Son of Man is he that sowed the good seed. It appears, then, that in the days of our Savior was the time of seeding: it was the time of sowing the word of life and dispensing it among the children of men. Sometimes the Savior, in view of the immediate results of that word in a limited sense, says to his disciples, “Say not ye, There are yet four…
The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.
And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.
For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.
And unto him that repenteth and sanctifieth himself before the Lord shall be given eternal life.

And the Lord thy God will make thee plenteous in every work of thine hand, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle, and in the fruit of thy land, for good: for the Lord will again rejoice over thee for good, as he rejoiced over thy fathers:
And whosoever doeth this must receive his wages of him; therefore, for his wages he receiveth death, as to things pertaining unto righteousness, being dead unto all good works.
For behold, the Lord shall come, and his recompense shall be with him, and he shall reward every man, and the poor shall rejoice;
And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.
And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.
But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully.
And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
¶ For thus saith the Lord to the men of Judah and Jerusalem, Break up your fallow ground, and sow not among thorns.
I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.
¶ Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.
And I have given you a land for which ye did not labour, and cities which ye built not, and ye dwell in them; of the vineyards and oliveyards which ye planted not do ye eat.
Man goeth forth unto his work and to his labour until the evening.
¶ And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did.
And there arose certain, and bare false witness against him, saying,
And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
And many believed on him there.
So when the Samaritans were come unto him, they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days.
And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.
But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
When he slew them, then they sought him: and they returned and inquired early after God.

Since he was about to leave them very 
shortly, therefore in this discourse He says 
nothing painful. But how? He extols as 
great the things which would be painful: all 
but saying, “‘ Fear not’: for ye shall receive 
power, after that the Holy Ghost is come 
upon you; and ye shall be witnesses unto Me 
both in Jerusalem, and in all Judea, and in 
Samaria.” For since he had said, “ Go not 
into…
And many more believed because of his own word;
And it came to pass that he did teach these things so much that many did believe on his words, even so many that they began to support him and give him money.
As he spake these words, many believed on him.
And as many as would hear his word he did teach. And he taught them privately, that it might not come to the knowledge of the king. And many did believe his words.
And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.
And said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.
And she said, Oh my lord, as thy soul liveth, my lord, I am the woman that stood by thee here, praying unto the Lord.
The woman saith unto him, I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things.
¶ Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
He left Judæa, and departed again into Galilee.
And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.
¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.
When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
¶ And it came to pass, as he went to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
For Jesus himself testified, that a prophet hath no honour in his own country.
But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.
And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.
And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.
Yea, and behold, if he had not been a prophet he could not have testified concerning those things.
Yet he sent prophets to them, to bring them again unto the Lord; and they testified against them: but they would not give ear.
Then when he was come into Galilee, the Galilæans received him, having seen all the things that he did at Jerusalem at the feast: for they also went unto the feast.
And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood afar off, beholding these things.

And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judæa, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?
(Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.
So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.
And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.
¶ And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all manner of sickness and all manner of disease among the people.
And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.
When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judæa into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.
This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judæa into Galilee.
¶ Now when Jesus had heard that John was cast into prison, he departed into Galilee;
Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.
But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.
And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him, If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;
And I said unto him: Believest thou the scriptures? And he said, Yea.
The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.
And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.

And he said, My son shall not go down with you; for his brother is dead, and he is left alone: if mischief befall him by the way in the which ye go, then shall ye bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
It shall come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die: and thy servants shall bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
And I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,

And he said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Because thou hast let go out of thy hand a man whom I appointed to utter destruction, therefore thy life shall go for his life, and thy people for his people.
And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.
And his brethren also went and fell down before his face; and they said, Behold, we be thy servants.
Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
And the mother of the child said, As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. And he arose, and followed her.
Then inquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.

And it came to pass that they went immediately, obeying the message which he had sent unto them; and they went in unto the house unto Zeezrom; and they found him upon his bed, sick, being very low with a burning fever; and his mind also was exceedingly sore because of his iniquities; and when he saw them he stretched forth his hand, and besought them that they would heal him.
When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
And it came to pass, that after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the doctors, both hearing them, and asking them questions.
And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.
So the father knew that it was at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and himself believed, and his whole house.
And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.
And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.

Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judæa into Galilee.
This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory; and his disciples believed on him.
¶ After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judæa; and there he tarried with them, and baptized.
